name: Putting up Your Preserves
recipeIngredient: 8oz glass jars with two part tops (lids with screw bands)
Rigid plastic containers or glasses with tight-fitting lids
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the jars, lids, and containers in an automatic dishwasher with very hot rinse water, keeping them hot until ready to use.
2. Alternatively, wash the jars and lids with hot, soapy water and rinse thoroughly.
3. Place the jars upside down in a large saucepot filled with water, bring to boiling and boil for 10 minutes. Keep them hot.
4. Wash two-part lids again in an automatic dishwasher with very hot rinse water or with hot, soapy water and rinse well.
5. Place the lids in a small saucepot filled with water and bring to a boil. Remove from water but keep hot until ready to use.
6. Fill jars with cooked jams to within 1/4-inch of the top, or no-cook freezer jams to within 1/2 inch.
7. Use a plastic knife or spatula to remove air bubbles from the jam.
8. Wipe jar rims and screw threads with a clean damp cloth.
9. Cover with lids and screw down tightly.
10. Invert jars for 5 minutes, then turn upright.
11. Allow cooked jams to stand at room temperature for 1 hour before refrigerating or freezing.
12. Allow no-cook jams to stand at room temperature for 24 hours before refrigerating or freezing.
13. Gently stir no-cook jams before refrigerating or freezing.
14. All jams must be frozen or refrigerated; store in the freezer for up to 1 year. Thaw frozen jams in the refrigerator and consume within 3 weeks.
